notes: Official Sber/OpenSLR-distributed Russian ASR corpus; public download is clear but license obligations still need audit.
Official SOVA page says the dataset is free and CC-BY-4.0, but the headline hours mix Russian and English rather than a clean Russian-only total.
Official Sber speech-emotion corpus with transcripts and public access, but reusable training terms still need audit.
OpenSLR corpus based on LibriVox public-domain audiobooks; clean small seed component.
Mozilla public corpus branch used in the released recipe.
Best clean extension of the Common Voice branch beyond the smaller CV12 slice NVIDIA used.
Strong multilingual benchmark-style Russian set; better for evaluation than scale.
Useful domain-shift benchmark, but the noncommercial/no-derivatives license needs review before training use.
Large umbrella corpus candidate, but provenance, overlap, and transcript quality need a deeper audit before counting it as clean public Russian ASR hours.
Small read-speech supplement with likely upstream audiobook overlap and custom licensing.
Single-speaker TTS-style Russian resource; interesting, but weak fit for broad ASR reconstruction.
Tiny spontaneous-speech stress-test set, not a meaningful scale source.

notes: Large paid Russian mobile-speech option if the public path stalls.
Telephony-style paid option for closer channel coverage.
